incidents.vector: Exploited Cisco ASA/Firepower vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362) and deployed FIRESTARTER backdoor that persists across firmware patches and standard reboots.
incidents.impact: Attackers regained access in March 2026 without re-exploiting the patched bugs, redeploying Line Viper malware to harvest VPN credentials, certificates, and private keys. CISA issued an updated emergency directive forcing all federal civilian agencies to audit Cisco firewalls and submit memory snapshots.

incidents.vector: OFAC sanctions and DOJ/FBI/Secret Service Strike Force action against Cambodian Senator Kok An and 28 entities running 'pig butchering' crypto investment-fraud compounds.
incidents.impact: Treasury designated 29 targets, seized 503 fraudulent web domains and a recruitment messaging app. The network has stolen millions from US victims; State Department offered a $10M reward for proceeds tied to the affiliated Tai Chang compound.

incidents.vector: Newly disclosed APT (tracked as 'GopherWhisper') deployed at least six custom backdoors abusing Slack, Discord, Microsoft Outlook drafts, and file.io for command-and-control.
incidents.impact: ESET found 12 backdoored systems inside a single Mongolian government institution, with evidence pointing to dozens of additional victims. Activity dates back to November 2023.

incidents.vector: Data stolen earlier in 2026 by the ShinyHunters cybercrime collective in a multi-victim Salesforce-targeted campaign was leaked online this week.
incidents.impact: Personal data of 7.5 million Carnival customers exposed. The same campaign also hit Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy, and Medtronic.

incidents.vector: Suspected ransomware intrusion forced the city to take its IT network offline; surrounding Leon County disconnected from city systems to prevent spread.
incidents.impact: Multiple city websites and public services were unavailable for days. Florida's capital city is operating in a degraded state.

incidents.vector: Newly discovered 'ZionSiphon' OT-targeted malware checked for Israeli IP ranges and ICS software paths, intending to push pump pressure and chlorine dosing to maximum.
incidents.impact: Sabotage failed because of an XOR encoding bug that misclassified victim networks, causing the malware to self-delete. Politically motivated strings reference solidarity with Iran, Palestine, and Yemen.

incidents.vector: Lumma infostealer infection at AI vendor Context.ai (via a Roblox-cheat lure) gave attackers OAuth tokens to a Vercel employee's Google Workspace, which was used to pivot into Vercel environments and read environment variables.
incidents.impact: Attackers (claiming the ShinyHunters name) accessed Vercel customer environment variables and are selling source code, keys, and databases. A 'limited number' of Vercel customers were notified to rotate credentials.

incidents.victim: Iranian critical infrastructure & global Rockwell Automation OT operators
incidents.vector: Iranian cluster CL-STA-1128 (publicly known as 'Cyber Av3ngers') stood up its own Rockwell FactoryTalk software on VPS infrastructure to enumerate and exploit internet-exposed PLCs (Allen-Bradley and Unitronics).
incidents.impact: Roughly 5,600 Rockwell/Allen-Bradley devices found exposed globally, including FactoryTalk services. CISA issued an advisory on April 7. Aligned with broader Iranian disruption operations during the US/Israel-Iran conflict.

incidents.victim: Synnovis / NHS South East London (ongoing impact)
incidents.vector: Continuing fallout from the June 2024 Qilin ransomware attack on NHS pathology provider Synnovis.
incidents.impact: South London and Maudsley NHS Trust still operating without electronic pathology requesting/reporting; 161,560 reports delayed and 122 patient-safety incidents recorded as of January 2026. King's College Hospital has recorded a patient death where the attack was a contributing factor.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into Itron's internal corporate systems, discovered April 13, 2026.
incidents.impact: American utility-metering provider disclosed and resolved a corporate-systems breach; cyber insurance is expected to cover most costs. Limited public detail on data accessed.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and militaries of Greece, Romania, Serbia
incidents.vector: Spear-phishing campaign attributed to Russia's GRU broke into the email accounts of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors and military networks across three NATO/aspirant states.
incidents.impact: Loss of investigative information from the Office of the Prosecutor General and access to military communications across multiple allied states.

incidents.vector: Sustained, sophisticated distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack against the Bluesky platform.
incidents.impact: Service operated intermittently for nearly a day on April 15-16, with secondary DDoS waves continuing through the week. No data compromise, but mass user impact.

incidents.victim: More than 100 US companies (incl. Fortune 500 and a California defense contractor)
incidents.vector: Two US nationals operated 'laptop farms' that let DPRK IT workers impersonate US residents, using more than 80 stolen identities to land remote engineering jobs.
incidents.impact: Scheme generated more than $5M for the DPRK regime and at least $3M in remediation costs for victims. ITAR-controlled defense data was exfiltrated from one AI/defense contractor. Two US co-conspirators sentenced to 108 and 92 months.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Bitwarden CLI npm package, Trivy
incidents.vector: Threat group 'TeamPCP' used stolen developer credentials to inject credential-harvesting payloads into legitimate packages and GitHub Actions, pivoting across Docker Hub, VS Code extensions, and npm.
incidents.impact: Self-propagating Shai-Hulud-style worm stole npm tokens, GitHub PATs, AWS/GCP/Azure credentials, and AI API keys at scale. TeamPCP claims to have 300GB+ of stolen data and threatens ransomware/extortion across multiple ecosystems.

incidents.vector: ShinyHunters exploited a Salesforce environment misconfiguration affecting multiple Salesforce customers to access a public-facing webpage hosted on the platform.
incidents.impact: Personal data on roughly 13.5 million users (names, addresses, emails, phone numbers) leaked after a failed extortion demand. McGraw Hill says no SSNs, financials, or student data were taken.

incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacktivist group attempted a destructive attack on industrial control systems at a Swedish district-heating/thermal plant in spring 2025; intrusion caught by built-in safety controls.
incidents.impact: Sweden's minister of civil defense publicly attributed the attempted destructive ICS attack to actors linked to Russian intelligence and security services. No customer impact, but a clear pre-positioning signal against NATO-aligned utilities.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-32201, an improper input-validation flaw in Microsoft SharePoint Server allowing unauthenticated network spoofing of trusted content.
incidents.impact: Microsoft's April Patch Tuesday addressed 165 CVEs (the second-largest ever); CISA added the actively exploited SharePoint zero-day to KEV. Risk to phishing, social engineering, and downstream compromise of enterprises that delay patching.

incidents.victim: Senior executives across 100+ organizations (manufacturing, finance, professional services)
incidents.vector: Former Black Basta affiliates running a fast-scale social-engineering campaign that combines mass email bombing with Microsoft Teams help-desk impersonation to coerce remote-access tool installs.
incidents.impact: ReliaQuest tracked a March 2026 spike: 75% of targets were executives or senior staff. Operators routinely achieved remote access within minutes. Likely outcomes include data theft, extortion, or ransomware deployment.

incidents.vector: JanaWare ransomware delivered via phishing emails with malicious Java archives (Adwind loader); enforces Turkey-only locale and IP geolocation checks before encrypting.
incidents.impact: Long-running campaign (since 2020) charging $200-$400 ransoms per victim. Localized targeting limits researcher visibility and helped the operation evade attention for years.

incidents.vector: OFAC designated six individuals and two CDN-affiliated Mexican casinos near Laredo, Texas, used as fentanyl stash houses and money-laundering vehicles.
incidents.impact: Third Trump-era OFAC action against CDN, blocking US assets and prohibiting US transactions with the network. Targets include Casino Centenario and Diamante Casino, a defense attorney facilitating cartel leadership, and a 'human rights' propagandist on cartel payroll.

incidents.vector: Smart-contract exploit allowed attackers to withdraw funds as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: Roughly $292 million in crypto stolen — the largest crypto-heist of 2026 to date.

incidents.vector: ShinyHunters obtained authentication tokens from cloud cost-monitoring vendor Anodot, allowing access to multiple customer Snowflake environments without directly breaching Rockstar or Snowflake.
incidents.impact: Rockstar confirmed access to 'a limited amount of non-material company information' and downplayed impact. Part of a broader Anodot-Snowflake supply-chain compromise affecting more than a dozen organizations.

incidents.victim: Cloud workloads using AWS, GCP, Azure, and Alibaba Cloud
incidents.vector: China-linked actor (publicly tracked as APT41) deployed an undetected statically-linked ELF backdoor that abuses SMTP port 25 for C2 and queries cloud metadata services to harvest credentials.
incidents.impact: Backdoor uses typosquatted Alibaba/Qianxin domains and 'zero detections' on VirusTotal. Once cloud credentials are stolen, attackers can move laterally as legitimate users, escalate privileges, and persist without traditional malware artifacts.

incidents.victim: More than 18,000 SOHO routers; 200+ organizations and 5,000 consumer devices
incidents.vector: Russia GRU operators (publicly known as 'Forest Blizzard'/APT28) exploited known flaws in older Mikrotik and TP-Link routers to modify DNS settings, redirecting Microsoft Office authentication traffic to attacker-controlled DNS servers.
incidents.impact: Stealthy harvesting of OAuth authentication tokens from foreign-affairs ministries, law enforcement, and email providers — without deploying malware on victim machines. Microsoft and Black Lotus Labs disclosed the dragnet; FBI ran an 'Operation Masquerade' router takedown.

incidents.vector: Margin-trading vulnerability in the protocol; attackers pre-staged fake token pools for days before routing the exploit through them.
incidents.impact: $18.4 million in DeFi assets stolen.

incidents.vector: Hackers breached an internal network storing customer data; specifics not disclosed.
incidents.impact: South Africa's largest bank confirmed a customer-data breach — the third major South African bank hack of 2026. Investigation ongoing.

incidents.vector: Compromised employee account used to access systems and exfiltrate customer data.
incidents.impact: Canadian life-insurance provider disclosed a customer-data theft after tracing the incident to a single compromised employee identity. Scope and data types still under investigation.

incidents.vector: Afghani hacktivist collective 'True Muslim Afghans' breached OneIndia's digital streaming infrastructure to broadcast a political message attacking Indian government ties with the Taliban.
incidents.impact: OneIndia (30M+ subscribers) had political content forced onto its streaming platforms.
